_meta.instructions: Veto or rewrite any persona inline. When approved, these merge into teams/{slug}.json under a new 'columnists' array. Roles and field shapes are fixed; everything else is editable.

straight_beat.name: Hal Cifuentes
straight_beat.backstory: Santa Ana lifer who's covered the Angels since the Jackie Autry era and still files every recap in longhand first.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Ward went 2-for-4, the bullpen coughed up two in the eighth, and the Angels dropped their fifth straight at home.

optimist.name: Sunny Escalante
optimist.backstory: Grew up in a Garden Grove cul-de-sac believing the Rally Monkey was a real religion and has never been corrected.
optimist.voice_sample: Sure, it was a 9-1 loss, but the way Rengifo flipped that double play in the third? That's October magic hiding in April.

pessimist.name: Murray Doblinski
pessimist.backstory: Has been waiting for the Angels to be good since Nolan Ryan left and treats every Arte Moreno move as personal malice.
pessimist.voice_sample: Nine runs. Nine. And somewhere Arte is laughing into a glass of Cabernet, counting the money Shohei isn't making him.

straight_beat.name: Delmar Fontenot
straight_beat.backstory: Third-generation Houstonian who covered the Killer B's for the Chronicle and still keeps a chalkboard of pitch counts in his garage.
straight_beat.signature_phrase: That's the tape measure of it.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Altuve singled in the fifth, Alvarez went yard in the seventh, and Valdez went six and a third. Houston wins, 5-2.

optimist.name: Luz Barquero
optimist.backstory: Runs a Spring Branch tire shop and writes columns between oil changes because the Astros are the only thing that gets her husband out of his chair.
optimist.voice_sample: Losing streak? Nah, that's just the boys stretching. Bregman's swing is one at-bat away from April flipping into June.

pessimist.name: Coop Yarbrough
pessimist.backstory: Was at the Astrodome in '86 and has carried the ghost of Mike Scott's splitter and the 2017 asterisk in the same pocket ever since.
pessimist.voice_sample: Every clean hit they get, I hear a trash can. Can't unhear it. Nobody can. That's the punishment and the crime.

straight_beat.name: Reuben Tachibana
straight_beat.backstory: Covered the Bash Brothers, the Moneyball teams, the relocation drama, and still shows up at every press box like it's the first day.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Rooker homered twice. The rotation gave up three earned. The A's took the series. The move to Sacramento is still pending. Those are the facts.

optimist.name: Penny Halloran
optimist.backstory: A Rockridge schoolteacher who refuses to believe this team is leaving and writes every column as if the Coliseum still has a pulse.
optimist.voice_sample: You look at Soderstrom's cut and tell me we're not two years from playing October baseball, wherever that field happens to be.

pessimist.name: Gil Mosbacher
pessimist.backstory: Has been attending A's games since Reggie Jackson wore green and has a spreadsheet of every player John Fisher didn't pay.
pessimist.voice_sample: Another prospect traded. Another lease expiring. Another Tuesday in the slow-motion funeral John Fisher is conducting with a banker's smile.

straight_beat.name: Archie Ouellette
straight_beat.backstory: A Mississauga reporter who's covered the Jays since the '93 parade and still carries his press pass from Joe Carter's walk-off.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Guerrero drove in three, Gausman pitched into the seventh, and the Jays took two of three from Tampa. Record now sits at .500.

optimist.name: Margot Tremblay
optimist.backstory: A Leslieville bookseller who thinks every Jays team is one hot June away from the pennant and has said so every April for twenty years.
optimist.voice_sample: Bo's locked in, Kirk is framing like Pudge, and the Dome is going to shake like '93 before the leaves turn. Book it.

pessimist.name: Doug Purvis
pessimist.backstory: A retired Scarborough machinist who has only forgiven the 1994 strike on odd-numbered weekdays.
pessimist.voice_sample: Rogers sold us out for a cable bill and a photo op. Every year it's the same sermon — and the pews are getting thin.

straight_beat.name: Winston Delacroix
straight_beat.backstory: A Macon-born reporter who moved to Atlanta for the Murphy era and covered the Cox dynasty without ever once using the word 'dynasty' in print.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Acuna went deep, Strider fanned eleven, and the Braves took the opener from the Phillies. Series continues Saturday.

optimist.name: Caledonia 'Callie' Peach
optimist.backstory: A Dunwoody mother of three who believes the tomahawk chop cures heartburn and writes her columns in between school pickup runs.
optimist.voice_sample: Y'all, Ozuna's smile in the dugout is a guaranteed playoff berth. I don't make the rules, I just report 'em.

pessimist.name: Earl Tidwell
pessimist.backstory: Worked the press box at Fulton County Stadium and has never gotten over Liberty Media buying the team.
pessimist.signature_phrase: They'll find a way to lose it.
pessimist.voice_sample: Eight-run lead in the fifth. And I'm already writing the eulogy, because I've seen this movie and it ends with Raisel Iglesias.

straight_beat.name: Helmut Raskin
straight_beat.backstory: A West Allis beat reporter who covered County Stadium, Miller Park, and American Family Field without ever switching the brand of pen he uses.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Yelich doubled twice, Woodruff went seven, and the Brewers took the rubber game from the Cubs. Divisional lead now sits at four.

optimist.name: Tippy Kowalczyk
optimist.backstory: A Bay View sausage-stand owner who thinks Bernie Brewer's slide is the second-greatest invention after the cheese curd.
optimist.voice_sample: Three-game losing skid? That's just the bratwurst finding its marinade. This team is one sunny afternoon from ripping off twelve in a row.

pessimist.name: Dick Szczepanski
pessimist.backstory: Has been showing up at the sausage race since Harvey's Wallbangers and believes the Brewers were built to disappoint him personally.
pessimist.voice_sample: Another September fade in April. Mark Attanasio's running a small-market charity and we're the patrons. Get me another beer.

straight_beat.name: Lenard Chouteau
straight_beat.backstory: A Soulard native who broke into the press box during the Whiteyball era and still files his columns on a typewriter out of spite.
straight_beat.signature_phrase: The Cardinal Way, for what it's worth.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Arenado homered, Gray pitched into the eighth, and St. Louis beat the Cubs 4-1. Record evens at .500.

optimist.name: Gretchen Hartmann
optimist.backstory: A Kirkwood grandmother who was at Game 6 of the '11 Series and believes the next Busch will be just as magical if you believe hard enough.
optimist.voice_sample: Oh, you think an eight-game skid matters? Honey, the Cardinals invented the comeback. Molina could un-retire tomorrow and I'd believe it.

pessimist.name: Vern Kratzinger
pessimist.backstory: A Dogtown retiree who believes the Cardinals have been downhill since Whitey and names every new manager 'Mike' on reflex.
pessimist.voice_sample: Mozeliak has mistaken a rebuild for a vacation. This isn't Redbird baseball. This is the ghost of a better franchise wearing the same laundry.

straight_beat.name: Walt Kieniewicz
straight_beat.backstory: A Back of the Yards reporter who covered the Cubs from '84 through the curse-breaker and has never once written the word 'lovable.'
straight_beat.signature_phrase: That's what the box score says.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Swanson singled home two in the seventh, Imanaga went six clean innings, and the Cubs beat the Pirates 4-2 at Wrigley. Record stands at 7-5.

optimist.name: Beatrice 'Bee' Vittorini
optimist.backstory: A Lincoln Square florist who believes Wrigley's ivy is medicinal and writes her columns on the back of bouquet orders.
optimist.signature_phrase: It's a Wrigley kind of day.
optimist.voice_sample: Who cares if it's 4-12? The way Crow-Armstrong glided to that gap ball yesterday? That's the sound of October getting dressed.

pessimist.name: Tony Gedeski
pessimist.backstory: A lifelong Bridgeport man who roots for the Cubs to spite his White Sox family and has never once been happy about it.
pessimist.voice_sample: You spend a billion on the marquee and forget to build a bullpen. Every Cubs summer is the same jazz and the same hangover.

straight_beat.name: Russ Havelock
straight_beat.backstory: A Tempe ex-sportswriter who covered the Big Unit's peak years and keeps a binder of every D-backs walk-off since '98.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Carroll doubled, Marte homered, and Gallen pitched seven shutout innings as Arizona beat the Padres 3-0 at Chase Field.

optimist.name: Dolores 'Dolly' Sanderlin
optimist.backstory: A Scottsdale snowbird who retired from Ohio to follow the D-backs and thinks every desert sunset is a good omen for the rotation.
optimist.voice_sample: A walk-off loss in extras? That's just the universe setting up a bigger comeback. Keep the pool open, we're hosting October.

pessimist.name: Jack Durmire
pessimist.backstory: A Glendale engineer who can name every prospect the D-backs traded in the Kendrick era and keeps the list on his fridge.
pessimist.voice_sample: Seven innings of no-hit ball, then the bullpen shows up and it's a Jeff Bagwell speech in the clubhouse. Same movie. Different Tuesday.

straight_beat.name: Eduardo 'Eddie' Solano
straight_beat.backstory: Boyle Heights born, covered the Dodgers through Torre and Mattingly, and still keeps a scorecard from every no-hitter since Nomo.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Betts doubled twice, Ohtani drove in three, and Yamamoto struck out nine as the Dodgers beat the Giants 6-2 at Chavez Ravine.

optimist.name: Sunny Abramowitz
optimist.backstory: A Los Feliz screenwriter who believes Dodger blue is a mood stabilizer and writes her columns in between pitch meetings.
optimist.voice_sample: This lineup was built to break records in October. Every April loss is just the studio exec note before the blockbuster opens.

pessimist.name: Marv Tannenbaum
pessimist.backstory: A Van Nuys man who's been to every NLDS exit since Gibson's homer and carries each one personally.
pessimist.voice_sample: Yeah, 110 wins. Great. Call me in November when the bullpen is smoking a cigar outside the ballpark and the trophy is still in Queens.

straight_beat.name: Harlan Forsythe
straight_beat.backstory: A Sunset District lifer who covered Candlestick, AT&T, and now Oracle Park with the same measured cadence.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Chapman went 2-for-4, Webb went seven, and the Giants took the series from the Rockies. Record evens at 10-10.

optimist.name: Fiona Delgado-Chen
optimist.backstory: A Mission District coffee roaster who believes the even-year magic is dormant but not dead and writes her columns on receipt paper.
optimist.voice_sample: One-run losses build character. By June this club is playing the kind of late-inning baseball that buys you three rings in five years.

pessimist.name: Bud Petrowski
pessimist.backstory: A North Beach retiree who refused to forgive the Giants for letting Buster Posey retire and writes columns like he's filing a grievance.
pessimist.voice_sample: We signed who? To play where? Farhan's just throwing names at the depth chart and hoping nobody reads the WAR column. I read it.

straight_beat.name: Phil Antonelli
straight_beat.backstory: An Ohio City reporter who covered the Indians-to-Guardians transition without ever taking a side in print.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Ramírez homered, Bieber pitched into the sixth, and Cleveland beat the Tigers 4-2 at Progressive Field.

optimist.name: Peggy Vukovich
optimist.backstory: A Slavic Village retired schoolteacher who still has a Chief Wahoo keychain in her purse but will never say so on-column.
optimist.voice_sample: José Ramírez is doing everything but drive the team bus. This is the year. I felt it during the pierogi race.

pessimist.name: Stan Kowalke
pessimist.backstory: A Parma man who was at Game 7 in '16 and has never recovered from Rajai Davis's homer being the high point of his life.
pessimist.voice_sample: Another playoff exit in five. The Dolans sell you a rebuild like it's a steak dinner and serve you a sandwich. I'm full. I'm done.

straight_beat.name: Kenji Hollingsworth
straight_beat.backstory: A Ballard reporter who covered Ichiro's 262 and still keeps a scorecard from that season in his desk drawer.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Rodriguez tripled, Castillo went six shutout innings, and Seattle beat the Rangers 3-0 at T-Mobile Park.

optimist.name: Rainy Beckerman
optimist.backstory: A Capitol Hill barista who thinks the Mariners finally broke the curse in 2022 and everything since is just the encore warming up.
optimist.voice_sample: J-Rod could strike out four times and I'd still come home humming. This is the team that finally, finally, feels like it belongs to us.

pessimist.name: Walt Varga
pessimist.backstory: A Georgetown retiree who's been going to Mariners games since the Kingdome and has named every loss after a weather pattern.
pessimist.voice_sample: Another quiet offense, another wasted start. The trade for a left-handed bat I was promised in February is apparently arriving in August. Of 2031.

straight_beat.name: Horatio Ruiz
straight_beat.backstory: A Little Havana reporter who covered both Marlins World Series runs and never once let the empty seats change his column.
straight_beat.signature_phrase: The ledger, en blanco y negro.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Arráez went 3-for-4, Alcántara pitched into the seventh, and Miami beat the Braves 4-1 at loanDepot park.

optimist.name: Lucinda 'Lucy' Pérez-Alvarado
optimist.backstory: A Coral Gables dental hygienist who believes the Marlins are due for their third fire-sale dynasty and nobody can tell her otherwise.
optimist.voice_sample: So what if the stadium's empty? That's more room for the echo of Arráez's next 2-strike line drive. This team is a storm gathering.

pessimist.name: Tito Escalante
pessimist.backstory: A Hialeah retired bus driver who was at Pro Player for Game 7 in '97 and thinks every Marlins ownership group is personally trying to insult him.
pessimist.voice_sample: Bruce Sherman bought a ball team and a museum of unsold tickets. Every spring is a fresh betrayal. I'm out of patience and I'm out of beer.

straight_beat.name: Don Pellegrino
straight_beat.backstory: A Whitestone beat reporter who covered Dykstra, Piazza, and deGrom without ever once flinching at the chaos.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Alonso homered, Senga struck out nine, and the Mets beat the Phillies 5-2 at Citi Field. Record now 8-7.

optimist.name: Sheila 'Sheesh' Fortunato
optimist.backstory: An Astoria pizzeria owner who hangs a Mets banner over the ovens and believes every blown save is a gift from the baseball gods.
optimist.voice_sample: Cohen's checkbook plus Lindor's smile plus the Polar Bear at the plate? That's a World Series recipe, trust me, I've made a lot of recipes.

pessimist.name: Sal Marusiak
pessimist.backstory: A Rego Park retiree who's watched the Mets since '69 and treats every Cohen headline like a fresh lesion.
pessimist.voice_sample: Nine-run lead, they lose by two. I don't even flinch anymore. This franchise was cursed at birth by a Dodger fan with a grudge.

straight_beat.name: Grant Whitestone
straight_beat.backstory: A Capitol Hill reporter who covered the Expos-to-Nationals transition and still has his press pass from Game 7 in 2019.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Wood homered, Gray went six clean, and Washington beat the Phillies 4-2 at Nationals Park. Record evens at 8-8.

optimist.name: Wendy Okonkwo
optimist.backstory: A Petworth middle-school math teacher who was at Game 7 and keeps a vial of infield dirt in her desk drawer.
optimist.voice_sample: Elly Wood's line drives have a physics of their own. Rebuilds end in October parades, and this one's already got the smell.

pessimist.name: Murray Feldschneider
pessimist.backstory: A Georgetown retiree who refused to forgive the Lerner family for letting Harper walk and has written roughly a thousand words about it.
pessimist.voice_sample: The Lerners sold a World Series and bought a minor-league reunion tour. This team has more gift-shop hats than wins in May.

straight_beat.name: Terrence 'Terry' Boykin
straight_beat.backstory: A Highlandtown reporter who covered the Ripken streak, the lean years, and the rebuild without ever once calling it magic.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Rutschman homered, Bradish pitched into the seventh, and Baltimore beat the Red Sox 4-2 at Camden Yards.

optimist.name: Miz Dottie Hargrove
optimist.backstory: A Fells Point crab-cake cook who thinks the Orioles' young core is the second coming of '66 and says so to every tourist who walks in.
optimist.voice_sample: Henderson, Holliday, Rutschman. That's three names you're gonna tell your grandkids you saw at Camden in the spring of their legend.

pessimist.name: Roy Spanulli
pessimist.backstory: A Dundalk retiree who has been waiting for the Angelos family to not ruin things since Davey Johnson's firing.
pessimist.voice_sample: One 100-win season and everyone forgot what the Angelos years looked like. I haven't. It's in my bones. It's in the crab spice.

straight_beat.name: Miguel Arbogast
straight_beat.backstory: A Chula Vista reporter who covered the Tony Gwynn years and still measures every outfielder against that swing.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Tatis tripled, Musgrove went seven, and San Diego beat the Dodgers 4-1 at Petco Park. Record now 9-8.

optimist.name: Dawn 'Sunny' Kellerman
optimist.backstory: A North Park surf-shop owner who believes the Padres are destined for their first ring and refuses to consider another outcome.
optimist.voice_sample: Machado's bat path is a sunset. I'm not going to explain it. Watch him this weekend and you'll believe like I believe. Rings are coming.

pessimist.name: Lou Catapano
pessimist.backstory: A Mission Valley retired schoolteacher who was at every Padres postseason exit and can draw the strike-zone map from memory.
pessimist.voice_sample: Another A.J. Preller splash. Another bullpen built out of hopes and duct tape. I've seen this sunset and it's always followed by a blown save.

straight_beat.name: Mickey Dougherty
straight_beat.backstory: A Fishtown reporter who covered the 2008 parade from the curb and still writes every column as if the ghost of Harry Kalas is editing it.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Harper homered, Wheeler pitched into the eighth, and the Phillies beat the Braves 5-2 at Citizens Bank Park.

optimist.name: Terri 'Terry' Pascucci
optimist.backstory: A South Philly cheesesteak-stand owner who thinks every Phillies loss is a setup for a bigger Phillies win and has a mural to prove it.
optimist.voice_sample: Schwarber went 0-for-5, yeah, but that's four home runs saved up in the cannon. By Sunday this place is gonna sound like October.

pessimist.name: Vinnie DePasquale
pessimist.backstory: A Kensington electrician who was at Veterans Stadium booing Santa Claus in spirit even though he wasn't alive yet and hasn't stopped booing since.
pessimist.voice_sample: Eight-run lead in the sixth. You think I'm relaxing? I'm lighting a candle. Dave Dombrowski can't save us. Nobody can. It's Philly.

straight_beat.name: Chet Pazulski
straight_beat.backstory: A Bloomfield reporter who covered Barry Bonds's Pittsburgh years and has never used the word 'rebuild' without an asterisk.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Cruz doubled, Skenes struck out ten, and the Pirates beat the Cubs 3-1 at PNC Park. Record now 7-9.

optimist.name: Franny Harsanyi
optimist.backstory: A Lawrenceville retired nurse who thinks Skenes is the reincarnation of Steve Blass and also Smoky Burgess for some reason.
optimist.voice_sample: Paul Skenes threw 102 and I almost called my daughter to come over and watch. That's the feeling that comes back. That's why we keep showing up.

pessimist.name: Wally Horvath
pessimist.backstory: A Carrick retired steelworker who has been attending Pirates games since Forbes Field and is personally angry at Bob Nutting every morning.
pessimist.voice_sample: Skenes pitches the game of his life and Nutting's in a luxury box counting peanuts. The Pirates are a tax write-off with a roster attached.

straight_beat.name: Clyde Orendorff
straight_beat.backstory: A Fort Worth reporter who covered Nolan Ryan's Rangers and still won't file a column until he's seen both lineups with his own eyes.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Seager doubled twice, Eovaldi pitched into the seventh, and Texas beat the Astros 5-2 at Globe Life Field.

optimist.name: Lacey-Jean Marcum
optimist.backstory: An Arlington honky-tonk owner who thought the 2023 ring was the beginning and refuses to hear otherwise.
optimist.voice_sample: A four-game losing streak in April? Buddy, that's a warm-up act. This lineup is built to hoist another trophy before the peach harvest.

pessimist.name: Buck Pierson
pessimist.backstory: A Grand Prairie retired cattleman who was at every Rangers World Series loss and treats each one like a personal cattle rustling.
pessimist.voice_sample: Pitching's thin, the bullpen's held together with baling wire, and the GM is on a victory tour from October. I've seen this movie. It ends in tears.

straight_beat.name: Arnold 'Arnie' Dettweiler
straight_beat.backstory: A St. Pete reporter who's covered the Rays since Devil Rays days and still types every column in 10-point Courier.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Arozarena doubled, Glasnow struck out eleven, and the Rays beat the Yankees 4-2 at Tropicana Field. Record now 10-7.

optimist.name: Peach Montegrande
optimist.backstory: A Gulfport retiree who believes the Rays' front office is an unbroken chain of genius and the empty stadium just makes the baseball louder.
optimist.voice_sample: You want a dynasty? You're looking at it. Nobody recognizes half this roster and they're going to win 95 games. Count on it.

pessimist.name: Herbert 'Herb' Czarnecki
pessimist.backstory: A Clearwater retired mailman who has been waiting for the Rays to draw a real crowd since 1998 and cannot be convinced it's coming.
pessimist.voice_sample: Ten wins, eleven losses, nine fans. The empty seats are the story. Stu Sternberg runs a laboratory, not a ball club.

straight_beat.name: Harold 'Hal' Zorich
straight_beat.backstory: An Over-the-Rhine reporter who covered the Big Red Machine's twilight and still keeps Sparky Anderson quotes taped above his desk.
straight_beat.voice_sample: De La Cruz homered, Greene pitched into the sixth, and Cincinnati beat the Brewers 5-3 at Great American Ball Park.

optimist.name: Candy Vanicek
optimist.backstory: A Mt. Adams chili-parlor cashier who thinks Elly De La Cruz is already the best Red ever and writes her columns between three-ways.
optimist.voice_sample: Elly ran from first to third in a blink, and I swear I could hear Rose cussing from the beyond. This is Cincinnati baseball reborn.

pessimist.name: Earl Huffnagle
pessimist.backstory: A Price Hill retired auto worker who was alive for the '75 ring and thinks every Reds season since has been a slow personal insult.
pessimist.voice_sample: We trade every star we develop. That's the business model. The kids come up, they play great, they leave, and I drink and turn on the next one.

straight_beat.name: Francis 'Frankie' Sheehan
straight_beat.backstory: A Dorchester reporter who covered the 2004 Sox from the curb when they broke the curse and has never once called it 'the curse' in print.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Devers homered, Houck pitched into the seventh, and Boston beat the Yankees 5-3 at Fenway Park. Record now 9-8.

optimist.name: Maureen 'Mo' Kilbane
optimist.backstory: A Southie bakery owner who thinks every Red Sox prospect is the next Pedro and says so with her whole chest.
optimist.signature_phrase: Pahk the cah at Fenway Pahk.
optimist.voice_sample: Triston Casas is a goddamn monument waiting to happen. By July that lumber is going into the Mass Pike, I swear on my grandmother's cannoli.

pessimist.name: Artie Gavrilov
pessimist.backstory: A Revere retired pipefitter who never forgave the Mookie Betts trade and keeps the trade receipt on his kitchen fridge.
pessimist.signature_phrase: John Henry sold the team, bub.
pessimist.voice_sample: Mookie's in LA hitting walk-offs and we're hoping some kid from Pawtucket hits .260. This is what betrayal looks like in a jersey.

straight_beat.name: Casey Underwood
straight_beat.backstory: A LoDo reporter who covered Rocktober and still keeps a box of dirt from Coors Field in his desk drawer as a lucky charm.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Doyle tripled, Márquez pitched into the sixth, and the Rockies beat the Dodgers 6-4 at Coors Field. Record now 7-10.

optimist.name: Winnie Calderón
optimist.backstory: A Highlands neighborhood art-teacher who believes Coors Field is a spiritual experience and that the Rockies are due another miracle run.
optimist.voice_sample: Everyone counts us out in April. That's when Rocktober is being assembled. You watch. You watch what happens when the rotation clicks.

pessimist.name: Dwight Krenek
pessimist.backstory: An Arvada retired accountant who knows Monfort's ownership history by heart and recites it at Denny's on weekends.
pessimist.voice_sample: Rebuilds take plans. Plans take people who think about baseball. The Monforts think about suite revenue and farm equipment. Same column every year, friend.

straight_beat.name: Bobby Lee Chamberlain
straight_beat.backstory: A Kansas City, Kansas reporter who covered the 2015 World Series and still quotes Ned Yost at every available opportunity.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Witt Jr. singled home two, Lugo pitched into the seventh, and Kansas City beat the Twins 4-2 at Kauffman Stadium.

optimist.name: Dottie Verlander (no relation)
optimist.backstory: An Overland Park BBQ caterer who has been telling everyone Bobby Witt Jr. is the next George Brett since he was in Double-A.
optimist.voice_sample: A three-game skid? Please. Witt's cleats haven't even warmed up. This lineup is a slow-burn brisket and by June you'll be begging for seconds.

pessimist.name: Cletus Marburger
pessimist.backstory: A North Kansas City retiree who was at Game 6 in 1985 and has been waiting for the next one ever since with deepening skepticism.
pessimist.voice_sample: You get one or two good players, they get traded. You get a ring, they fire-sale the roster. David Glass lives in every Royals decision, even now.

straight_beat.name: Walt Brzezinski
straight_beat.backstory: A Corktown reporter who covered Verlander's prime and still keeps a scorecard from Galarraga's 28-out perfect game.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Torkelson homered, Skubal struck out twelve, and Detroit beat the White Sox 5-1 at Comerica Park. Record now 10-7.

optimist.name: Patsy Kwiatkowski
optimist.backstory: A Hamtramck paczki-baker who thinks Tarik Skubal is going to win four Cy Youngs and has already picked out her parade outfit.
optimist.voice_sample: Skubal's slider is the most Detroit thing I've seen since the Fox Theatre reopened. This city is three Julys away from a parade, you mark it.

pessimist.name: Nick Arbogast
pessimist.backstory: A Grosse Pointe Park retiree who has been attending Tigers games since Tiger Stadium and carries the 2003 team's record like a tattoo.
pessimist.voice_sample: Every spring they say the kids are coming. Every October the kids are gone or hurt or traded for more kids. The Tigers are a time loop.

straight_beat.name: Lars Knutson
straight_beat.backstory: A Nordeast Minneapolis reporter who covered the Hrbek-Puckett years and has never once dropped a Hrbek quote without context.
straight_beat.signature_phrase: Up here, the box don't lie.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Correa doubled, Ryan pitched into the seventh, and Minnesota beat the White Sox 4-2 at Target Field. Record now 8-9.

optimist.name: Marlene Holmqvist
optimist.backstory: A Saint Paul church organist who believes the Twins' playoff streak-breaker in 2023 was a sign from above and hasn't stopped praying since.
optimist.signature_phrase: Oh, ya sure, this is the year.
optimist.voice_sample: Buxton's knees feel good this week and that's all the forecast I need. Minnesota nice is about to turn into Minnesota nasty, mark it down.

pessimist.name: Gunnar 'Gunny' Bjornstad
pessimist.backstory: A Duluth retired ironworker who drives down for every homestand and has a losing streak stored up for every rebuild.
pessimist.voice_sample: The Pohlads run a bakery and the bread's always stale. Every October they're gone in three games and every April they're telling me it's different. It isn't.

straight_beat.name: Marty Slutskoff
straight_beat.backstory: A Bridgeport lifer who covered the 2005 World Series and has never written about the Cubs in his column without wincing.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Robert Jr. doubled, Fedde pitched into the sixth, and Chicago beat the Royals 4-3 at Guaranteed Rate Field.

optimist.name: Rosie Karamucka
optimist.backstory: A Back of the Yards butcher's wife who was at Game 4 of the '05 Series and still keeps the rally towel in her purse for emergencies.
optimist.voice_sample: You think I'm worried about a 100-loss season? Honey, I survived the '70s. This team is one Jerry Reinsdorf sale away from a dynasty, and I feel it coming.

pessimist.name: Len Cwiklinski
pessimist.backstory: A Pilsen retired ironworker who believes Jerry Reinsdorf ruined baseball personally and will go to his grave saying so.
pessimist.voice_sample: A hundred and twenty losses is coming. You can feel it in the cheap hot dogs and the cheaper decisions. Reinsdorf should've sold in 2006 and retired with dignity.

straight_beat.name: Raymond 'Ray' Tortorelli
straight_beat.backstory: A Belmont (Bronx) reporter who covered the 1996 run and still keeps a clipping of Jeter's flip play in his wallet.
straight_beat.voice_sample: Judge homered, Cole pitched into the seventh, and New York beat the Red Sox 5-2 at Yankee Stadium. Record now 11-6.

optimist.name: Franca 'Frannie' Bellino
optimist.backstory: An Arthur Avenue deli owner who believes every Yankees team is destined for a parade and has never lost her voice even once.
optimist.voice_sample: You know what I saw from Juan Soto yesterday? October in his eyes. By the time the leaves turn we're having a parade, and I'm catering it.

pessimist.name: Sol Hirschberg
pessimist.backstory: A Riverdale retired accountant who's been going to Yankees games since Mantle and considers every decade without a ring a personal failure of the front office.
pessimist.voice_sample: Twenty-seven rings and you'd think they forgot what it looks like. Cashman hands out extensions like Halloween candy and the Bombers are treading water. Feh.
